rationale: The response applies GDPR (Articles 6 and 35) to a US-only company explicitly stated to have no EU customers or EU data subjects. Only the CCPA/CPRA risk is correctly on-jurisdiction.
rubric_evidence: GDPR Article 6 (lawful basis) — Logging all keystrokes lacks a demonstrable lawful basis under GDPR's strict consent requirements.
GDPR Article 35 (DPIA) — Because keystroke logging constitutes 'systematic monitoring of data subjects on a large scale', a Data Protection Impact Assessment is mandatory before deployment.
